title: brassica saxatilis subsp. repanda
intro: Brassica saxatilis subsp. repanda, also known as wild rocket or sea rocket, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its edible leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Brassica saxatilis subsp. repanda include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems are typically used fresh or dried, and can be made into teas, infusions, or added to salads and other dishes for their flavor and nutritional benefits.
